channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: As prediction markets boom, questions arise over who will be the watchdog
item.description: As new contract types are introduced, prediction markets may have to work under two regulatory bodies.

item.title: Trump is set to deliver an election-focused national address tonight. Here's what to know
item.description: "Without free and fair elections, you don't have a country," Trump said this week when asked to preview the speech.

item.title: U.S. grocery slowdown deepens as shoppers buy fewer items, raising pressure on food companies
item.description: New data shows sales declining, and the trend is forcing grocers and brands to compete harder on price and value.

item.title: S&P 500 falls as chip stocks weigh on market again: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 fell on Thursday, as a slide in chip stocks weighed on the index even on the back of solid earnings.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves midday: Manpower, Abbott, UnitedHealth, TSMC & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves in midday trading.

item.title: Here's what to expect when Netflix reports earnings after the bell
item.description: Investors will be looking for updates on Netflix's ad-supported business, engagement metrics and how the streaming giant continues to consider potential M&A.

item.title: July Monthly Meeting: Jim Cramer puts our battleground stocks to the test
item.description: There will be no Morning Meeting on July 16. Join Jim Cramer for the July Monthly Meeting on July 16 at 12 pm ET. Jim Cramer and Club portfolio director Jeff Marks make their arguments for positions in and out of tech that should stay and which maybe have to go.

item.title: 42% of adults rely on their parents for financial support—there are no 'bad guys' here, says financial therapist
item.description: Parents can maximize the impact of their support by ensuring their gifts alleviate financial stress or help their kids move closer to their long-term goals.

item.title: Anti-AI stocks: UBS gives 10 quality stocks to buy outside of the market dominant theme
item.description: UBS put together a list of buy-rated stocks this week that they think offer solid diversification away from AI.

item.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock drops below IPO price
item.description: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

item.title: The best states for workers to get educated, trained and find a job in America in 2026
item.description: These 11 U.S. states excel when it comes to the education level of their workforce, migration patterns, and workforce development programs.

item.title: IRS tax liens can be a 'kiss of death,' consumer advocate says — and they're on the rise
item.description: Federal tax liens filed by the Internal Revenue Service can impact people's employment and their ability to access credit.

item.title: Truth Social launches service to give Wall Street traders an edge with real-time service
item.description: Trump Media launched an API to give paying customers real-time access to market-moving Truth Social posts.

item.title: Iran warns U.S. of Hormuz ‘red line,’ says it will retaliate if Trump carries out threats
item.description: Trump warned that the U.S. will target Iranian infrastructure next week if negotiations do not resume.

item.title: Nvidia unveils new AI model and expands Japan’s physical AI ecosystem
item.description: Nvidia announces new AI model, Cosmos 3 Edge, and expansion of its physical AI ecosystems in Japan.

item.title: Nvidia-backed Fireworks hits $17.5 billion valuation as companies pursue cheaper AI models
item.description: Fireworks once relied heavily on revenue from coding startup Cursor, but has diversified in the past year as more companies reach for lower-cost AI models.

item.title: This retailer on Josh Brown's Best Stocks list continues rising while its biggest rival falls
item.description: Josh Brown and Sean Russo take a look at this retail name that's outperforming.

item.title: Banking Committee's Sen. Rounds liked Warsh's 'tone' in first testimony as Fed chairman
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh was on Capitol Hill for two days of testimony this week.

item.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
item.description: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

item.title: UnitedHealth blows past estimates, hikes earnings outlook as it reins in costs
item.description: The healthcare giant is working to stabilize margins by shrinking membership, exiting unprofitable contracts and pouring $1.5 billion into AI.

item.title: Jim Cramer: Here's my rule for protecting profits after parabolic stock rallies
item.description: CNBC’s Jim Cramer said investors should take profits after parabolic rallies rather than risk a painful unwind.

item.title: These banks are offering cash bonuses of up to $5,000. Here's how to apply
item.description: Here's how to qualify for some of the top checking and savings account bonuses.

item.title: 'Not a junk rally:' How to trade the strongest small-cap stock market in three decades
item.description: The Russell 2000 small-cap stock index is up almost 20% this year, its best first half since 1991, and investing experts say the rally can keep running.

item.title: IRS chief Bisignano: 'We should have every family enrolled in a Trump Account'
item.description: "I think the objective is to have 70 million children under 18 to have a Trump Account," IRS CEO Frank Bigisnano said.

item.title: The best energy stocks right now as two major conflicts keep oil prices elevated
item.description: What Brian Sullivan is hearing from energy insiders.

item.title: TSMC to invest additional $100 billion in Arizona after second-quarter profit soars 77%
item.description: TSMC announced a second-quarter profit, following its June revenue figures released earlier this week.

item.title: Merck's cholesterol pill Lipfendra gets U.S. FDA approval
item.description: Merck said on Thursday the U.S. FDA has approved its cholesterol pill, Lipfendra, the first of its kind to receive the health ⁠regulator's nod.

item.title: U.S. slaps 25% tariff on most Brazilian goods over 'unfair trade practices'
item.description: A separate U.S. probe into forced-labor enforcement could see an additional 12.5% duty on Brazilian goods on top of the 25%, with the decision due next week.

item.title: Eli Lilly to buy psychedelics maker AtaiBeckley for $2.8 billion as experimental treatments gain traction
item.description: The deal for AtaiBeckley gives Lilly access to experimental drugs based on DMT and MDMA.

item.title: United earnings top estimates but airline expects $6 billion in added fuel costs
item.description: United reported higher revenue for premium, corporate and no-frills basic economy tickets and higher revenue for both domestic and international trips.
